description: Verification overrides for AI App Builders competitive analysis

dreamflow::gh_native.note: Built-in Source Control panel. Connect via repo HTTPS URL + Personal Access Token (PAT). Once connected, Dreamflow tracks file changes and supports common Git operations inside the UI (branching, committing/pushing, pulling/merging, conflict resolution, disconnect).

evidence.title: Dreamflow Git Integration - Full Workflow Support
evidence.snippet: Documentation states: "Dreamflow includes a built-in Source Control panel that supports common Git operations including branching, committing/pushing, pulling/merging, and conflict resolution. Connect via repo HTTPS URL + Personal Access Token (PAT)." This confirms native Git integration with full workflow support directly in the UI.

lovable::gh_native.note: GitHub becomes the single source of truth once connected. Two-way sync is supported: edits in Lovable appear in GitHub, and edits in GitHub appear back in Lovable. Connection depends on stable repo path (org/account/repo name).

evidence.title: Lovable GitHub Integration - Two-Way Sync
evidence.snippet: Documentation explicitly states: "Single source of truth: When connected, your code lives in GitHub, not separately in Lovable. Two-way sync: Edits in Lovable appear in GitHub, and vice versa." Further details: "Sync automatically: Edits in Lovable appear in GitHub, and changes in GitHub sync back on the default branch (main)." This confirms native GitHub integration with bidirectional synchronization.

vibecode::gh_native.note: No native GitHub integration documented; export/SSH workflow instead.

evidence.title: Vibecode Export/SSH - No Native GitHub Integration
evidence.snippet: Documentation describes: "SSH allows you to connect your Vibecode projects directly to code editors like Cursor. This gives you a secure, direct connection to your code so you can edit, deploy, and manage your app from your preferred development environment." No mention of native GitHub integration in product; instead uses export/SSH workflow. Confirmed by absence of GitHub integration in docs.

builder::gh_native.note: Repo-connected workflow designed around pull requests. Changes are packaged and submitted as PRs from inside the product (Send PR), reviewed in a Pull requests tab, and further edits can be requested by commenting on the PR and tagging @builderio-bot.

evidence.title: Builder.io GitHub Integration - PR-Native Workflow
evidence.snippet: Documentation states: "Builder Projects connects directly to your repositories, letting you visually edit code and create applications with AI assistance." Further details: "To submit changes and create a pull request (PR) to the connected repository: In the Visual Editor, make the required updates. Click Send PR from the toolbar. In the Pull requests tab, review the proposed changes. To request additional edits, comment in the pull request and tag @builderio-bot with specific instructions." This confirms native GitHub integration with PR-centric workflow.

bolt::gh_native.note: GitHub integration with frequent auto-commits and regular fetch. Bolt creates a commit whenever a change 'doesn't break the project', and checks GitHub periodically (docs mention every 30 seconds) to pull in external updates.

evidence.title: Bolt.new GitHub Integration - Auto-Commit System
evidence.snippet: Documentation explicitly states: "Bolt integrates directly with GitHub, handling all commit and update operations automatically." Details on auto-commit: "Bolt saves your work automatically. Every time you make a change that doesn't break the project, Bolt creates a commit for you. It also checks GitHub every 30 seconds for any updates made outside Bolt and pulls those in." This confirms native GitHub integration with automatic synchronization.

dreamflow::import_repo.note: Supports importing existing Flutter repos via 'Clone Codebase'. Limitations include Flutter-only (non-Flutter/monorepo may fail), and projects that require local servers/background processes or private dependencies may fail. Codegen scripts can't be executed inside Dreamflow; generated files must be produced externally and committed.

evidence.title: Dreamflow's Flutter Repo Import Limitations
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'Dreamflow currently supports Flutter repositories only. If your repository contains non-Flutter code or multiple projects (like a monorepo), the import may fail.' It also mentions, 'Projects requiring local API servers or background processes during development will fail to run inside Dreamflow.' Additionally, 'You cannot currently execute scripts inside of the Dreamflow builder. If the imported project depends on code generation, you will need to generate Dart files outside of Dreamflow and commit them to the repo.' These quotes confirm the limitations described in the claim.

lovable::import_repo.note: Lovable FAQ: importing existing repos is not supported.

evidence.title: Lovable does not support importing existing GitHub repos
evidence.snippet: "Can I import an existing GitHub repo into Lovable? No. You can only export from Lovable to GitHub, not the other way around." This quote explicitly states that importing existing GitHub repositories into Lovable is not supported, confirming the claimed status.

evidence.title: Feature Not Documented in Vibecode.app
evidence.snippet: "Page Not Found" and "We couldn't find the page." - The documentation does not contain any information about the unknown feature, as indicated by multiple 'Page Not Found' errors. This supports the claim that the feature is not documented.

builder::import_repo.note: Connects to existing repositories as the basis for Projects. Intended for working against real codebases with PR-based delivery to the repo (not an export-only model).

evidence.title: Builder.io Connects to Git Repositories for Projects
evidence.snippet: The documentation states, 'Builder Projects connects directly to your repositories, letting you visually edit code and create applications with AI assistance.' It further explains, 'To connect an existing Git repository: On the Projects page, click Connect Repo. Connect to your Git provider, such as GitHub, Azure DevOps, or Bitbucket and grant access to all or selected repositories.' This supports the claim that Builder.io connects to existing repositories as the basis for Projects.

bolt::import_repo.note: Docs explicitly include an 'Import an existing repository' flow as part of the GitHub integration. Bolt then syncs via commits/fetch (rather than PR creation inside the tool).

evidence.title: Bolt's GitHub Integration Supports Importing Repositories
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'To import a project into Bolt from one of your GitHub repositories, follow the steps below: ... Use the drop-down menu to select the repository you’d like to import, or Click the Import from URL button, then enter your GitHub repository URL.' This confirms that Bolt includes an 'Import an existing repository' flow as part of its GitHub integration.

dreamflow::create_repo.note: Connecting an existing Dreamflow project to Git requires a 'new blank repository' (repo must be blank or only contain README/LICENSE). Practically, repo creation happens on the Git provider side first, then you connect it in Dreamflow.

evidence.title: Verification of Git Connection Requirements in Dreamflow
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'To connect a Dreamflow project to Git repo: Open your existing project in Dreamflow. Go to the Source Control Panel on the left sidebar and click Connect Repository. In the dialog, provide: Repository URL – Paste the HTTPS URL of your new blank repository. If you haven’t already created one, you can create a new repository on your Git provider (such as GitHub) before proceeding.' This confirms that the repository must be blank or contain only a README and/or LICENSE file.

lovable::create_repo.note: Connect flow creates a repo in chosen org.

evidence.title: Lovable GitHub Integration Creates Repository
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'A new GitHub repository is created for your project, and sync begins automatically.' This confirms that when you connect a project to GitHub through Lovable, a repository is indeed created in the chosen organization.

evidence.title: Feature Absence in Vibecode Documentation
evidence.snippet: "Page Not Found" and "We couldn't find the page" indicate that the documentation for the feature is missing. This supports the claim that the feature is not documented, as the URLs provided lead to a 404 error page.

builder::create_repo.note: Repo creation implied but not explicit in our cited docs.

evidence.title: Repository Creation Feature Status
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'To connect an existing Git repository: On the Projects page, click Connect Repo.' This implies the ability to connect to existing repositories but does not explicitly mention the creation of new repositories.

bolt::create_repo.note: GitHub integration supports creating a new repo from a Bolt project (documented as part of the integration's workflows).

evidence.title: GitHub Integration for Creating Repositories
evidence.snippet: "Create a new repository from a Bolt project" - This section of the documentation explicitly outlines the steps to create a new GitHub repository from a Bolt project, confirming the feature's existence. It states: 'To create a repository from your Bolt project... enter a name for your new repository. Click Create repository.' This confirms the claimed feature.

dreamflow::branching.note: Branching is supported directly in the Source Control panel: create a branch, switch branches, and refresh branch list. Dreamflow can also pull changes and merge commits from the remote branch.

evidence.title: Dreamflow Git Branching Feature Verification
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'You can easily manage branches directly within the platform, i.e., create new ones, switch between existing ones, and refresh your branch list when needed.' Additionally, it mentions: 'Dreamflow will fetch and merge any new commits from your remote branch.' These quotes confirm that branching, switching branches, refreshing branch lists, and merging commits from a remote branch are supported.

evidence.title: GitHub Branch Switching as Experimental Feature
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'Enable experimental features like GitHub branch switching to test new capabilities before they become generally available.' This indicates that GitHub branch switching is indeed a Labs/experimental feature, supporting the claim that it is in a LIMITED status.

evidence.title: Feature Not Documented in Vibecode
evidence.snippet: The documentation page returns 'Page Not Found' and suggests that the page you were looking for does not exist. It lists alternative pages such as 'Native iOS UI Components' and 'Expo Push Notifications', but none related to the unknown feature.

builder::branching.note: Branch/PR workflow is supported. PR creation is the documented delivery mechanism ('Send PR'), and further edits can be requested via PR comments tagging @builderio-bot (adds commits to the same PR).

evidence.title: Builder.io Supports Branch/PR Workflow
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'To submit changes and create a pull request (PR) to the connected repository: In the Visual Editor, make the required updates. Click Send PR from the toolbar.' It further explains, 'To request additional edits, comment in the pull request and tag @builderio-bot with specific instructions. The bot applies the changes to the same PR.' These quotes confirm the support for a branch/PR workflow and the use of PR comments to request further edits.

bolt::branching.note: Branch switching is supported as part of GitHub integration. Merge is not described as an in-app operation (workflow expects merging in GitHub).

evidence.title: Branch Switching Supported, Merging Not In-App
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'You can create new branches in Bolt or on GitHub.' and 'Bolt currently doesn’t support merging branches in-app. You need to merge branches in GitHub.' These quotes confirm that branch switching is supported as part of GitHub integration, but merging is not an in-app operation.

evidence.title: Verification of Unknown Feature Claim in Dreamflow
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'Dreamflow supports Git integration, allowing you to connect your projects directly to GitHub, GitLab, or other Git providers.' However, there is no mention of any 'Unknown Feature' within the provided documentation, supporting the claim that it is 'Not documented.'

evidence.title: Absence of Unknown Feature in GitHub Integration
evidence.snippet: The documentation extensively covers the GitHub integration features, such as code backup, collaboration, and two-way sync. However, it does not mention any 'Unknown Feature'. This absence in the detailed documentation supports the claim that the feature is not documented.

evidence.title: Feature Not Documented in Vibecode.app
evidence.snippet: The documentation page returns 'Page Not Found' and states, 'We couldn't find the page.' This indicates that the feature is not documented, supporting the claim that it is absent.

builder::pr_creation.note: PR creation is explicitly supported: make updates, click 'Send PR', review in Pull requests tab. Additional edits can be requested by commenting on the PR and tagging @builderio-bot; the bot applies changes to the same PR as new commits.

evidence.title: Builder.io PR Creation and Editing Feature
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'To submit changes and create a pull request (PR) to the connected repository: In the Visual Editor, make the required updates. Click Send PR from the toolbar. In the Pull requests tab, review the proposed changes. To request additional edits, comment in the pull request and tag @builderio-bot with specific instructions. The bot applies the changes to the same PR.' This confirms that Builder.io supports PR creation and allows additional edits via @builderio-bot.

evidence.title: Unknown Feature Not Documented in Bolt
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention any feature labeled as 'Unknown Feature'. The provided documentation focuses on GitHub integration, including connecting GitHub, creating repositories, and managing branches, but makes no reference to an 'Unknown Feature'.

evidence.title: Absence of Unknown Feature in Dreamflow Git Documentation
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention any 'Unknown Feature'. The document covers Git integration, cloning codebases, connecting projects to Git, and Git operations, but there is no reference to an 'Unknown Feature'.

evidence.title: No Documentation of Unknown Feature in Lovable
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention any feature beyond the GitHub integration capabilities. The text states, 'Can I import an existing GitHub repo into Lovable? No. You can only export from Lovable to GitHub, not the other way around.' This indicates that the feature in question, which is unknown and claimed to be undocumented, is indeed not present in the available documentation.

evidence.title: Feature Not Documented in Vibecode.app
evidence.snippet: The documentation pages for Vibecode.app are largely inaccessible, with multiple 'Page Not Found' errors. The available sections like 'Introduction', 'Use Cases', and 'Features' do not provide any details about the unknown feature, as evidenced by the repeated '404 Page Not Found' messages.

evidence.title: Absence of GitLab Workflow Merge Feature
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'Follow these steps to work with your connected repository: Make changes to the repository with Projects. Click Send PR to create a pull request. Review and merge the pull request in your GitLab instance.' This indicates that while Builder.io allows creating pull requests, the actual merging of workflows must be conducted within the GitLab instance, not directly through Builder.io.

bolt::merge_in_product.note: Merging is not an in-tool workflow; docs position GitHub as the place to merge. Bolt focuses on commits + fetch rather than merge actions inside Bolt.

evidence.title: Bolt Lacks In-App Merging Feature
evidence.snippet: "Bolt currently doesn’t support merging branches in-app. You need to merge branches in GitHub." This statement directly confirms that Bolt does not have an in-tool workflow for merging branches, supporting the claim that merging is positioned to be done in GitHub instead.

dreamflow::multi_user_collab.note: Docs explicitly warn multi-user collaboration within the same repository is not supported. If multiple Dreamflow projects attempt to use the same repo, the system detects and restricts it; team collaboration is positioned as an Enterprise plan discussion.

evidence.title: Multi-user Collaboration Limitation in Dreamflow
evidence.snippet: "warning Currently, Dreamflow does not support multi-user collaboration within the same repository. If multiple Dreamflow projects attempt to use the same repo, the system will detect and restrict it. For team collaboration, please reach out regarding the Enterprise Plan." This quote confirms that Dreamflow explicitly warns against multi-user collaboration within the same repository and suggests that team collaboration is a feature discussed under the Enterprise plan.

lovable::multi_user_collab.note: Collaboration is framed around GitHub workflows because GitHub is the source of truth with two-way sync. The doc does not describe real-time multi-user editing inside Lovable itself; team flow is primarily via repo, branches, reviews, and stable repo path.

evidence.title: GitHub-Centric Collaboration in Lovable
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'Collaborate easily: Invite developers, use pull requests, branches, and code reviews.' It also mentions: 'Edits in Lovable appear in GitHub, and changes in GitHub sync back on the default branch (main).' These quotes confirm that collaboration is structured around GitHub workflows, supporting the claim that real-time multi-user editing within Lovable itself is not described.

evidence.title: Feature Not Documented in Vibecode.app
evidence.snippet: The documentation page returns a '404 Page Not Found' error, indicating that the specific feature documentation does not exist. The message 'We couldn't find the page. Maybe you were looking for one of these pages below?' further confirms the absence of documentation for the claimed feature.

evidence.title: PR-Centric Workflow for Teams in Builder.io
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'To submit changes and create a pull request (PR) to the connected repository: In the Visual Editor, make the required updates. Click Send PR from the toolbar.' This indicates that Builder.io supports a PR-centric workflow by allowing users to create pull requests directly from the Visual Editor.

bolt::multi_user_collab.note: Docs state Bolt doesn't support GitHub organization accounts (individual accounts only). Team collaboration therefore relies on GitHub-side workflows rather than org-wide integration inside Bolt.

evidence.title: Bolt GitHub Integration Limitations
evidence.snippet: The documentation states, 'Bolt currently doesn’t support GitHub organization accounts. Sign up for one if you don’t already have one.' This confirms that Bolt only supports individual GitHub accounts, not organization accounts, which aligns with the claim that team collaboration relies on GitHub-side workflows rather than org-wide integration inside Bolt.

evidence.title: Agent Panel in Dreamflow Workspace
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'Agent Panel: AI-powered development assistant that helps you build and modify your Flutter app through natural language conversations. Features streaming conversations, context management, screenshot integration, and support for multiple AI models. The agent panel is to the right of the workspace.' This confirms that the agent operates over the project workspace.

evidence.title: Documentation of Debugging Guidance in Lovable
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'Fix build errors, debug unexpected behavior, resolve preview issues, and recover from common problems step by step.' It also provides a step-by-step guide: 'Click the Try to Fix button when an error shows up. Lovable will scan your logs, detect the issue, and attempt a quick fix.' This indicates that Lovable offers debugging guidance.

vibecode::codebase_aware_prompting.note: Prompts apply to generated project; scope not clearly defined in docs.

evidence.title: Feature Documentation Not Found
evidence.snippet: The documentation page for Vibecode.app could not be found, as indicated by repeated 'Page Not Found' messages. This suggests that the documentation does not currently provide information on the feature in question.

builder::codebase_aware_prompting.note: AI assistance exists but prompting is not the primary documented interface.

evidence.title: AI Assistance in Builder.io
evidence.snippet: The documentation mentions 'Projects from Prompts' and 'AI Generate Content Instructions & Style Inspiration,' indicating AI assistance features. However, the primary interface for prompting is not emphasized, supporting the claim that prompting is not the main documented interface.

bolt::codebase_aware_prompting.note: Prompts operate over project/files in Bolt environment.

evidence.title: Lack of Evidence for Unknown Feature in Bolt
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention any feature where 'prompts operate over project/files in Bolt environment.' The sections on managing projects, duplicating projects, and using the chatbox do not reference such a capability.

evidence.title: Repo Context After Clone/Connect
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'Once connected, Dreamflow will automatically start tracking file changes, which you can view in the Changes list.' This indicates that after cloning or connecting a repository, it becomes part of the context for tracking changes within Dreamflow.

lovable::repo_as_ai_input.note: Docs: cannot import existing repo into Lovable.

evidence.title: Importing Existing GitHub Repositories Not Supported
evidence.snippet: "Can I import an existing GitHub repo into Lovable? No. You can only export from Lovable to GitHub, not the other way around." This quote directly confirms that Lovable does not support importing existing GitHub repositories, thus supporting the claimed status and note.

evidence.title: Feature Not Documented in Vibecode.app
evidence.snippet: The documentation page provided leads to a 'Page Not Found' error, as indicated by the text: 'Page Not Found Skip to main content Vibecode Docs home page'. This suggests that the feature in question is not documented, supporting the claim that the feature status is 'NO' and 'Not documented'.

builder::repo_as_ai_input.note: Connect existing repos; additional repos for context.

evidence.title: Builder.io Supports Connecting and Adding Repositories
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'To connect an existing Git repository: On the Projects page, click Connect Repo. Connect to your Git provider, such as GitHub, Azure DevOps, or Bitbucket and grant access to all or selected repositories.' Additionally, it mentions: 'To add repositories: Click the three dots on the project tile and choose Project settings. Expand Workspace Settings. Click the Add Repository button.' This confirms that Builder.io allows connecting existing repositories and adding additional ones for context.

evidence.title: Import Existing GitHub Repositories into Bolt
evidence.snippet: "To import a project into Bolt from one of your GitHub repositories, follow the steps below:... Use the drop-down menu to select the repository you’d like to import, or Click the Import from URL button, then enter your GitHub repository URL. Click Choose this repository. Bolt will load your repository as a new Bolt project." This confirms that Bolt supports importing existing repositories from GitHub, as claimed.

dreamflow::file_component_targeting.note: Not explicitly documented as a feature.

evidence.title: Feature Not Documented in Dreamflow
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention the 'Unknown Feature' as part of the Dreamflow workspace. The documentation covers various panels and functionalities, such as the Top Bar, Modules Panel, Content Panel, Properties Panel, Agent Panel, and Bottom Bar, but there is no mention of an 'Unknown Feature.'

evidence.title: Unknown Feature Not Documented
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention any 'Unknown Feature.' The sections cover troubleshooting, build errors, and general issues, but there is no reference to an 'Unknown Feature.'

evidence.title: Feature Not Documented in Vibecode.app
evidence.snippet: The documentation returns a 'Page Not Found' error, stating 'We couldn't find the page.' This indicates that the specific feature documentation is not available or does not exist within the provided URLs.

evidence.title: Feature Absence in Builder.io Documentation
evidence.snippet: The documentation provided does not mention the 'Unknown Feature'. The extensive list of features and capabilities outlined in the documentation does not include any reference to an 'Unknown Feature'.

evidence.title: Verification of Unknown Feature in Bolt.new
evidence.snippet: The documentation provided does not mention any 'Unknown Feature' related to Bolt.new. The documentation covers various aspects of managing projects, such as creating, renaming, deleting, and duplicating projects, but there is no reference to an 'Unknown Feature'.

evidence.title: Absence of Unknown Feature in Git Integration
evidence.snippet: The documentation details Git integration features such as 'Clone Codebase', 'Connect Project to Git', and 'Git Operations', but does not mention any 'Unknown Feature'. The absence of any reference to an 'Unknown Feature' supports the claim that it is 'Not documented'.

evidence.title: Absence of the Unknown Feature in Lovable Documentation
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention any 'Unknown Feature.' It focuses solely on the GitHub integration, detailing steps to connect, sync, and disconnect projects from GitHub. There is no reference to any other feature that could be considered 'Unknown.'

evidence.title: Feature Not Documented in Vibecode.app
evidence.snippet: "Page Not Found" and "We couldn't find the page." These quotes indicate that the documentation page for the feature is missing or does not exist, supporting the claim that the feature is not documented.

builder::diff_aware_prompting.note: Not documented as diff-aware prompting; PR workflow exists.

evidence.title: Absence of Diff-Aware Prompting Feature
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention 'diff-aware prompting' anywhere. It discusses features like connecting Git repositories, creating branches, and using pull requests, but there is no reference to diff-aware prompting. The documentation states: 'To submit changes and create a pull request (PR) to the connected repository: In the Visual Editor, make the required updates. Click Send PR from the toolbar.'

evidence.title: Absence of Unknown Feature in Bolt Documentation
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention any 'Unknown Feature'. The text primarily focuses on GitHub integration, including creating repositories, branching, and committing changes. There is no mention of any feature that could be interpreted as 'Unknown Feature'.

evidence.title: Verification of Unknown Feature Absence in Dreamflow
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention any 'Unknown Feature'. The sections cover various panels and functionalities such as the Top Bar, Modules Panel, Content Panel, Properties Panel, Agent Panel, and Bottom Bar, but there is no mention of an 'Unknown Feature'.

evidence.title: Absence of Unknown Feature in Documentation
evidence.snippet: The documentation provided does not mention any 'Unknown Feature'. It focuses on troubleshooting, build errors, and core functionalities, but there is no reference to an 'Unknown Feature'.

evidence.title: Feature Not Documented in Vibecode
evidence.snippet: The documentation page for Vibecode states 'Page Not Found' and 'We couldn't find the page.' This indicates that the specific feature documentation is missing or not available.

evidence.title: Verification of Unknown Feature Absence in Builder.io
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention or describe any 'Unknown Feature'. The extensive list of features and integrations, such as 'Figma to Code', 'Headless CMS', and 'AI Prototyping', does not include any unnamed or unspecified features. This supports the claim that the 'Unknown Feature' is not documented.

evidence.title: Unknown Feature Not Documented in Bolt
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention any 'Unknown Feature'. The sections cover various functionalities such as managing projects, duplicating projects, and working with databases, but there is no reference to an 'Unknown Feature'.

dreamflow::screenshot_prompt_input.note: Workspace supports visuals; not explicitly framed as prompt input.

evidence.title: Workspace Visuals Not Explicitly Framed as Prompt Input
evidence.snippet: The documentation describes the Workspace as 'organized into panels that work together to provide a complete development experience.' It mentions features like 'real-time app preview with device frame options' and 'integrated code editor with syntax highlighting and auto-complete,' but does not explicitly frame visuals as prompt input.

evidence.title: Screenshot Recommendation for Troubleshooting
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'UI or Layout Problems Provide screenshots of the UI issue directly to the AI for a clearer diagnosis.' This indicates that the troubleshooting guidance explicitly recommends using screenshots to help diagnose UI issues.

evidence.title: Feature Not Documented in Vibecode.app
evidence.snippet: The documentation page for Vibecode.app returns a 'Page Not Found' error and suggests that the page may not exist. The text states, 'We couldn't find the page. Maybe you were looking for one of these pages below?' This indicates that the feature is not documented.

evidence.title: Verification of Unknown Feature Absence

evidence.title: Feature Absence Confirmation in Dreamflow Documentation
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention any 'Unknown Feature'. The sections cover various panels and functionalities such as the Top Bar, Modules Panel, Content Panel, Properties Panel, and Agent Panel, but there is no reference to an 'Unknown Feature'.

These indicate the existence of workflows for converting Figma designs to code but do not explicitly mention a direct Figma to app import feature.

evidence.snippet: "Using Bolt’s Figma integration, you can quickly load Figma designs into Bolt, and generate sites and UIs based on the designs. Bolt uses Anima to handle turning Figma designs into apps." This confirms that Bolt supports importing Figma designs via Anima, as claimed.

evidence.title: Absence of Unknown Feature in Dreamflow Documentation
evidence.snippet: The documentation for Dreamflow's Workspace does not mention any 'Unknown Feature.' The sections covered include Workspace Modules, Content Panel, Properties Panel, Agent Panel, and more, but there is no reference to an 'Unknown Feature.'

evidence.title: Absence of Unknown Feature in Lovable Documentation
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention any 'Unknown Feature'. The sections covered include troubleshooting, build errors, and core functionalities, but there is no reference to an 'Unknown Feature'. This supports the claim that the feature is not documented.

evidence.title: Verification of Unknown Feature Absence in Bolt.new
evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention any 'Unknown Feature'. The documentation provides detailed information about managing projects, duplicating projects, and working with databases, but there is no reference to an 'Unknown Feature'.

evidence.title: Feature Not Documented in Dreamflow Workspace
evidence.snippet: The documentation for the Dreamflow Workspace does not mention an 'Unknown Feature'. The sections covered include Workspace Modules, Content Panel, Properties Panel, Agent Panel, and Keyboard Shortcuts, but there is no reference to an 'Unknown Feature'.

lovable::mcp_connectors.note: MCP servers documented (including custom servers + auth options).

evidence.title: Documentation of MCP Servers Including Custom Servers and Auth Options
evidence.snippet: The documentation states: 'Lovable includes several prebuilt personal connectors on all plans, with support for custom MCP servers on all paid plans.' It further explains: 'For full flexibility, you can connect custom MCP servers as personal connectors to bring internal or third-party systems into Lovable.' Additionally, it details authentication options: 'Authentication: OAuth (default)...Bearer token or API key...No authentication.' These quotes confirm that MCP servers, including custom servers and various authentication options, are documented.

evidence.snippet: The documentation does not mention any 'Unknown Feature'. The sections cover various functionalities like managing projects, duplicating projects, and integrating with other tools, but there is no reference to an 'Unknown Feature'.

evidence.title: Absence of Unknown Feature in Dreamflow Git Integration
evidence.snippet: The documentation extensively covers Git integration features such as connecting repositories, cloning codebases, and performing Git operations like branching, pushing, and pulling changes. However, there is no mention of an 'Unknown Feature'. This suggests that the claimed feature is indeed not documented.

evidence.title: Absence of Unknown Feature in Lovable Documentation

This confirms Git conflict handling features such as viewing diffs and resolving conflicts. However, there is no mention of build/test gating as an automatic pre-commit validation step.
